pattern: Counting odds in a range reduces to a closed-form count-up-to formula evaluated at both ends, so no per-number loop is ever needed even for huge ranges.
hints: Think about how many odd numbers exist from 0 up through some value x. Is there a direct formula rather than counting one at a time?
The count of odds up to x is (x + 1) // 2. Apply that idea to both ends of the interval.
Combine the two endpoint counts directly: (high + 1) // 2 minus low // 2 gives the odds strictly within [low, high].

edge_inputs.description: single even value has no odd numbers
near the maximum value with a tiny span
maximum value used for both endpoints
even low and odd high, off-by-one prone
range entirely made of odd numbers

stress.description: random small spans anywhere across the full magnitude range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    span = min(max(size, 0), 100000)
    low = rng.randint(0, 10**9 - span)
    high = low + span
    return [low, high]
